question: Based on the age vs. salary scatterplot, what can be reliably concluded about how salary varies with age?

options.A: Individuals in their early 50s clearly earn the highest salaries, well above all other age groups.
options.B: Salaries for people in their late 40s are clearly lower than those for people in their late 50s.
options.C: The chart does not make it clear which specific age group earns the highest salary because the points and labels are too small to distinguish precisely.
options.D: Individuals in their mid-30s clearly earn the highest salaries compared with all other age groups.

explanation: The individuals in the chart between the ages of 52 and 58 have the highest salary. The small size of the chart may cause difficulty in accurately identifying the specific trends of salary in certain age ranges.
